dataset.full_name: A longitudinal MRI dataset of brain metastases with tumor segmentations, clinical and radiomic data
dataset.description: Brain metastasis segmentation from multi-sequence MRI scans
dataset.acquisition_info: 1.5T and 3T MRI scanners (Philips, Siemens, Toshiba) with T1-weighted, T2-weighted, post-contrast T1-weighted (T1c), and FLAIR sequences. Contrast agent: Gadovist (0.1 mmol/kg). Slice thickness: 0.5-5.0mm, slice spacing: 0.5-6.5mm.
dataset.annotation_info: Initial segmentation using automated methods (DeepBraTumIA, nnU-Net) and semi-automated software (Imalytics Preclinical), followed by manual correction and refinement by an expert neuroradiologist with over 10 years of experience.
dataset.data_source: Multi-center study from 6 diagnostic centers in Cyprus, part of the PROTEAS project and aligned with BraTS standards.
dataset.detailed_description: This dataset is a subset of the PROTEAS longitudinal brain metastasis dataset, containing 44 patient cases with multi-sequence MRI scans. The imaging includes four standard sequences: FLAIR, T1-weighted, post-contrast T1-weighted (T1c), and T2-weighted. The segmentation task focuses on delineating three key tumor subregions of brain metastases: necrotic core, peritumoral edema, and enhancing tumor. All images were preprocessed and registered to the BraTS spatial framework to ensure standardization. The annotations were generated through a combination of automated and semi-automated methods with final expert neuroradiologist review.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate segmentation of brain metastasis subregions is crucial for treatment planning, particularly for stereotactic radiosurgery, and for monitoring treatment response during follow-up. Precise delineation of enhancing tumor, edema, and necrotic regions enables better assessment of tumor burden and therapeutic efficacy.
